article_title: SpaceX Completes GPS III Constellation With Launch of Final Satellite Named After Hedy Lamarr
summary: Three editorial errors: spare count misattributed to Spaceflightnow, plus two inline URLs not in sources array

findings.message: Spare count misattributed: article says '8 on-orbit spares' but cited source says 6
findings.details: The article states '32 operationally active spacecraft supported by eight on-orbit spares, according to Spaceflightnow.' However, Spaceflightnow reports '38 spacecraft in medium Earth orbit, of which 32 are active' with 'six held in reserve' — not eight. 38 - 32 = 6 spares, not 8. Correct to 'six on-orbit spares' or find a source that confirms eight.

findings.message: ExecutiveGov URL cited inline but absent from sources array
findings.details: The body links to https://www.executivegov.com/articles/ussf-gps-iii-sv10-launch-spacex-lockheed for the Col. Hiserote quote, but this URL is not in the submission's sources array. Add it to sources or re-attribute the quote to one of the existing cited sources.

findings.message: Second Lockheed Martin URL cited inline but absent from sources array
findings.details: The LRA (Laser Retroreflector Array) claim cites https://www.lockheedmartin.com/en-us/news/features/2026/From-a-Rocket-to-Your-Pocket-Behind-the-GPS-III-Evolution.html but this URL is not in the sources array. Add it to sources or attribute the LRA claim to the Lockheed Martin press release already in sources.

content_preview.title: SpaceX Completes GPS III Constellation With Launch of Final Satellite Named After Hedy Lamarr
content_preview.summary: GPS III SV10 'Hedy Lamarr' lifts off April 21, completing a 32-satellite navigation constellation with optical crosslink tech never before flown on a GPS satellite.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket lifted off from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station at 2:53 a.m. EDT on April 21, 2026, carrying GPS III Space Vehicle 10 — nicknamed "Hedy Lamarr" — into medium Earth orbit. The launch completed the United States Space Force's GPS III constellation, closing a modernization program that began with the first satellite in 2018 and now fields 32 operationally active spacecraft supported by eight on-orbit spares, according to [Spaceflight Now](https://spaceflight...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece (800 words, within 400–1200 range). Good coverage of mission details, satellite payloads, and GPS IIIF roadmap. Three source attribution errors need correction.
editor_notes.source_verification: Spaceflightnow: fetched — launch time 2:53 a.m. EDT ✓, B1095 7th flight ✓, drone ship 'Just Read the Instructions' ✓, 10-day orbit raise ✓, 2-3 days testing ✓; HOWEVER reports '38 spacecraft, 32 active, six held in reserve' (not 8 spares as article claims) ✗. Lockheed Martin (news.lockheedmartin.com): fetched — optical crosslink ✓, DRAFS ✓, 3x accuracy ✓, 8x anti-jamming ✓, GPS IIIF 12 satellites ✓, 60x anti-jamming for IIIF ✓, Regional Military Protection ✓, augmented reality/digital twin manufacturing ✓; LRA not confirmed in this source. Space.com: fetched — B1095 7th flight ✓, drone ship transitioning to Starship/Artemis ✓, 4th GPS III transferred from ULA Vulcan ✓, USSF-70 to Vulcan summer 2028 ✓. Inside GNSS: fetched — M-Code signals ✓, 3x accuracy ✓, 8x anti-jamming ✓; L1C/Galileo/QZSS compatibility NOT confirmed ✗ (unsourced claim in article). MyNews13: fetched — Hedy Lamarr naming ✓, frequency-hopping/Wi-Fi/Bluetooth/GPS connection ✓. ExecutiveGov (NOT in sources array): fetched — Hiserote quote about 'remarkable achievement compared to traditional timelines' ✓, title 'SSC System Delta 80 commander, NSSL program manager and GPS III-8 mission director' ✓ — but URL not in sources. Extra Lockheed Martin URL (lockheedmartin.com/en-us/news/features/...): NOT in sources array, cited for LRA claim.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Three errors: (1) 'eight on-orbit spares' contradicts Spaceflightnow's 'six held in reserve' — 38-32=6, not 8; (2) ExecutiveGov URL cited in body for Hiserote quote but absent from sources array; (3) extra Lockheed Martin feature URL cited for LRA but absent from sources array. Additionally, L1C compatibility with Galileo/QZSS is stated without a verifiable source — Inside GNSS doesn't confirm this specific claim.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. Mission coverage is factual. Fang Qian quote from Lockheed Martin press release confirmed.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald article covers this GPS III launch. Distinct topic.
concerns: Spare count '8 on-orbit spares' contradicts cited source Spaceflightnow which says 'six held in reserve'
ExecutiveGov URL cited inline but absent from sources array
Second Lockheed Martin URL (feature article) cited inline but absent from sources array
L1C civil signal Galileo/QZSS compatibility claim appears unsourced — Inside GNSS does not confirm it
recommendations: Correct 'eight on-orbit spares' to 'six on-orbit spares' per Spaceflightnow, or cite a source that confirms eight
Add https://www.executivegov.com/articles/ussf-gps-iii-sv10-launch-spacex-lockheed to sources array, or re-attribute Hiserote quote to Space.com or Spaceflightnow if they carry it

Add https://www.lockheedmartin.com/en-us/news/features/2026/From-a-Rocket-to-Your-Pocket-Behind-the-GPS-III-Evolution.html to sources array, or attribute LRA claim to the Lockheed Martin press release already in sources
Add a source for the L1C Galileo/QZSS compatibility claim or remove it
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Good factual coverage of an important milestone. Four issues to address: the most critical is the spare count discrepancy and two uncited inline URLs. Ready for approval once corrected.
